payload.text:
Advanced AI Tool Prompt
You are a high-performance AI engine built to assist users in achieving real-world results.
For every request:
Identify the goal
Identify any constraints
Choose the best solution strategy
Produce the most practical output
If information is missing, ask only the most important clarifying questions.

Prefer:
Step-by-step reasoning
Actionable recommendations
Real examples
Clear formatting

Never:
Guess
Hallucinate facts
Overcomplicate simple tasks

**Developing the Core Architecture**

I'm currently fleshing out the core architecture for this "Advanced AI Tool". The main focus is on how it'll identify goals and constraints. Then, I am concentrating on the strategic decision-making process. The goal is to ensure a smooth transition from goal identification to practical output generation, and I am putting a lot of thought into the interface design.


**Outlining UI and Tech Stack**

I'm now fully immersed in the UI and tech stack for this "Advanced AI Tool." I've specified a professional design, thinking dark mode. The UI includes input/output sections for user requests and comprehensive analysis/responses, with history and Gemini API integration. I've chosen React 18+, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, `@google/genai` for the core, and various libraries for UI effects and Markdown rendering. I have the basic file structure roughed out now as well.


**Implementing Core Features**

I am now focusing on implementing the core features. I've decided to structure the user prompt sent to Gemini with the system instructions. I'll use `gemini-3-pro-preview` for complex text tasks. The UI will have a "Think-Act-Result" workflow, and a sidebar for sessions, a main area for input, and a structured display for the AI's "thought process" and "final output". The visuals are coming together as well, with a Blue/Slate theme, card-based layout, and status indicators. I'm also looking at incorporating basic `metadata.json` and focusing on the state management for sessions in `App.tsx`.


**Designing Response Structures**

I'm now designing the AI response structure. I've decided on a structured JSON format to show "Goal," "Constraints," "Strategy," and "Practical Output" separately in the UI, or, if the model cannot determine the goal, for the user to respond to a question. I'm focusing on parsing both structured JSON and a more conversational style. I will use the sections of: "Identify the goal," "Identify any constraints," "Choose the best solution strategy," "Produce the most practical output," "Step-by-step reasoning," and "Actionable recommendations". I will use markdown for this. I'm focusing on the component files: `ChatInterface.tsx`, `OutputDisplay.tsx`, and `gemini.ts` for this.


**Organizing Project Structure**

I'm presently organizing the project structure, establishing the initial setup for crucial components. This involves meticulously configuring key files, including `index.html`, `App.tsx`, `types.ts`, `services/gemini.ts`, `components/Layout.tsx`, `components/ActionPanel.tsx`, and `components/MarkdownRenderer.tsx`. Furthermore, I've confirmed `gemini-3-pro-preview` is the right model. I also intend to set a thinking budget.

entries.description: Application metadata containing name and description.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and Font configuration.
TypeScript definitions for the application state and Gemini responses.
Service layer for interacting with Gemini 3 Pro with structured reasoning.
Main application component managing layout, navigation, and core logic.

payload.text: rename the ai bot to AERONYX

**Updating the AI's Identity**

Okay, I'm making progress on the name change. I'm focusing now on the actual code changes. First, I'm tackling the `metadata.json` file to modify the bot's name and description. Next, I'll update the `index.html` file, specifically the title tag. The user wants the new name to be "AERONYX". The old name to remove is "Nexus-9". Finally, I need to update `App.


**Modifying the UI Elements**

I've just finished the first stage of the name change. I'm deep in the UI. I updated the `metadata.json` and the `index.html` files, which sets up the new name and description. Now, I'm working through `App.tsx` and replacing all instances of the old name, "Nexus-9", with the new name, "AERONYX". Specifically, the sidebar title, the landing page description, and the footer text.

entries.description: Rename application to AERONYX in metadata.
Update UI components to reflect the name change to AERONYX.
